checks.finding: requirements.md REQ-002/REQ-003 enumerate the fixed 14-entry CLI allowlist, exact get_os_info fields, and per-entry available/version semantics; REQ-005 fixes version normalization to first line / 200 chars. acceptance-tests.md AC-001..AC-015 pin concrete expected outputs per tool and per error path. No requirement or AC leaves room for two reasonable implementers to diverge on behavior.

Cross-checked REQ-003 timeout/output-limit/parallelism values (2s/8KiB/4) against AC-004 (same values); REQ-007..009 default-bundling language against AC-009's --mcp sdd-forge-mcp exclusion case; Non-goals (no execution, no env-var values) against REQ-002/REQ-003 tool surface (version-string-only, execFile-only, no user-controlled args). All are mutually consistent; no conflicting requirement, AC, or non-goal pair found.

requirements.md 'Edge Cases' section lists missing CLI, hung/verbose probe, stderr-only version output, allowlist-external name, missing/corrupt IDE config file, and Node<20, each traced to AC-002, AC-004, AC-003, AC-010/AC-011, AC-015 with concrete expected behavior (per-entry available:false, kill+per-entry failure, invalid-input, skip+notify, error-without-overwrite). Material negative and boundary paths implied by the requirements are enumerated with expected behavior.

requirements.md Assumptions section's config-path assumption is escalated to OQ-001 with explicit Owner ('実装タスク担当(Cursor / VS Code 登録タスク)'), 'Blocks Implementation: no', and a stated Resolution Path (confirm official docs, then update design.md). The execution-scope interpretation assumption is resolved by reference to a named decision record (ADR-0004) marked for human approval. Neither assumption silently blocks the specification.

requirements.md Assumptions section states the allowlist/execution-scope interpretation is recorded as ADR-0004 and is subject to human approval -- a testable, named governance boundary. No other irreversible or governance boundary is implied by installer upsert/uninstall (REQ-008/009/010), which are reversible idempotent operations with their own AC coverage (AC-010/011/012/015), so no additional approval boundary is missing.

requirements.md names the contract artifact (contracts/local-env-mcp-tools.v1.schema.json), enumerates the error-code enum, tabulates Security Boundaries (B1-B3) with trust/PII/regulatory columns, and states Risks with a mitigation direction (static check + negative tests for allowlist injection). Non-goals bound scope precisely (no execution, no env-var values, no file reads). This gives implementation-policy review a bounded, concrete surface without requiring the specification to contain design or task-decomposition content.
